task: Write a Python function sum_distinct(nums) returning the sum of the distinct values in the list, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The submitted function correctly implements the sum of distinct values in a list.
trace: I utilized Python's built-in set data structure, which automatically removes duplicates when creating it from a list. The `sum()` function is then used to sum up the elements of the distinct set, ensuring that each value is counted once. To handle an empty list edge case, I return 0 instead of raising an error or returning an undefined value.
